summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several recent developments in small UAS activity. Ukraine’s drone operators have wiped out a Russian FPV drone crew on the Huliaipole front and struck Russian logistics 50 km behind the front line in Donetsk. Ukrainian engineers have developed the Chipa net gun to physically snare FPV drones when other air defenses fail. Russian forces have tested a white “penguin” camouflage suit in Ukraine, but at least two soldiers wearing it were located and neutralized by FPV drones within hours. The U.S. Army National Guard has purchased three types of FPV drones for military training, and Ukraine’s upgraded basic military training now emphasizes FPV drone defenses.
